content.problemStatement:
Chef bought a huge (effectively infinite) planar island and built NNN restaurants (numbered 111 through NNN) on it. For each valid iii, the Cartesian coordinates of restaurant iii are (Xi,Yi)(X_i, Y_i)(Xi​,Yi​).

Now, Chef wants to build N−1N-1N−1 straight narrow roads (line segments) on the island. The roads may have arbitrary lengths; restaurants do not have to lie on the roads. The slope of each road must be 111 or −1-1−1, i.e. for any two points (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1​,y1​) and (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2​,y2​) on the same road, ∣x1−x2∣=∣y1−y2∣|x_1-x_2| = |y_1-y_2|∣x1​−x2​∣=∣y1​−y2​∣ must hold.

Let's denote the minimum distance Chef has to walk from restaurant iii to reach a road by DiD_iDi​. Then, let's denote a=max (D1,D2,…,DN)a = \mathrm{max}\,(D_1, D_2, \ldots, D_N)a=max(D1​,D2​,…,DN​); Chef wants this distance to be minimum possible.

Chef is a busy person, so he decided to give you the job of building the roads. You should find a way to build them that minimises aaa and compute a⋅2a \cdot \sqrt{2}a⋅2​.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers XiX_iXi​ and YiY_iYi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one real number — the minimum distance aaa multiplied by 2\sqrt{2}2​. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute or relative error does not exceed 10−610^{-6}10−6.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We should build roads described by equations y−x+0.5=0y-x+0.5 = 0y−x+0.5=0 and y−x−0.5=0y-x-0.5 = 0y−x−0.5=0.
Example case 2: We should build roads described by equations y−x−1=0y-x-1 = 0y−x−1=0 and y+x−1=0y+x-1 = 0y+x−1=0.
